sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.38 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: A formation$F$is called radical in a class$H$\/ if$F H$and in every$H$-group the product of any two normal$F$-subgroups belongs to$F$. Let$M$be the class of all saturated hereditary formations of finite groups such that the formation of all finite supersoluble groups is radical in every element of$M$. Is it true that$M$has the largest (by inclusion) element? { A.\,F.\,Vasil'ev, L.\,A.\,Shemetkov}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
